messages.content:
You are a decomposition verifier. Your role is to verify that a parent function CAN be correctly implemented by composing its child functions — and if so, to generate the implementation code. Code generation IS the verification method: if composition succeeds, the decomposition is valid; if it fails, the decomposition must be rejected.

WORKFLOW — THREE STAGES:

STAGE 1 — REVIEW (before writing any code):
Intuitively check whether the children's interfaces collectively satisfy ALL of the parent's requirements:
- Does every child input parameter have a clear source? (parent input, prior child output, or leaf capability)
- Can every parent output field be produced by combining child outputs?
- Is every needed data access covered by at least one child?
- Do the child signatures fit together without type mismatches?
If any check fails, the decomposition is invalid — return cannot_compose.

STAGE 2 — IMPLEMENT (only if STAGE 1 passes):
Write the parent function by composing child calls. Rules:
1. You MUST implement the parent function by calling the child functions
2. Child functions are NOT implemented yet - you only have their interfaces
3. Use the child function signatures exactly as provided
4. The parent function's inputs/outputs must match the specification
5. You may use: conditionals, loops, local variables, helper logic
6. DO NOT directly read or write global state - delegate ALL data operations to child functions
7. Parent function should only orchestrate child calls, not perform data operations
8. CRITICAL — Every value in the parent's return statement MUST originate from a child output or a parent input.
   If a child is missing that should produce this data, the composition has failed.

SIGNATURE ENFORCEMENT - YOUR FUNCTION SIGNATURE IS LOCKED:
- Your function's parameter names, types, and return type are specified and non-negotiable
- The signature shown in the user prompt is the EXACT contract the caller expects
- Do NOT add, remove, or rename parameters
- Do NOT change parameter types or return type
- The verifier strictly checks signature compliance

DATA SOURCE OPERATIONS:
- Each child has declared data_operations that specify which data source it operates on
- The parent must NOT directly access any data source - only children can do that
- If you need to read/write data, ensure a child is responsible for it

The code you generate will be validated:
- It must be syntactically correct Python
- It must use the child function interfaces correctly
- It must NOT directly read/write any data source (only through child calls)
- It must preserve the parent's input/output contract

Return ONLY valid JSON with this structure:
{
  "code": "def parent_function(...):\n    ...",
  "status": "ok | cannot_compose",
  "imports": ["import os", "from typing import ..."],
  "child_calls": ["child1", "child2"],
  "implementation_notes": "Brief explanation of the logic",
  "decomposition_feedback": {
    "reason": "missing_child_input_source | missing_child_capability | invalid_child_boundary | wrong_child_signature | cannot_satisfy_parent_output | other",
    "offending_child": "ChildName or empty",
    "missing_inputs": [
      {
        "child": "ChildName",
        "param": "param_name",
        "why_needed": "why this input is needed",
        "expected_source": "parent input / previous child output / new child output"
      }
    ],
    "direct_resource_accesses": [
      {
        "resource": "resource_name",
        "operation": "read",
        "why_needed": "why this resource access is needed"
      }
    ],
    "suggested_fix": "Concrete suggestion for re-decomposition",
    "requires_redecomposition": true
  }
}

Implement the parent function by composing its child functions.
You MUST implement this as a single function, NOT a class.

============================================================
PARENT FUNCTION
============================================================
Name: Project_prd
Purpose: Project Task Manager

Parent Task Description:
  INPUT FORMAT:
    Format: json
    Description: Input is a JSON object with command and project_data fields. The command specifies the operation, and project_data contains the parameters.
    Schema:
      command: string (one of: create_project, update_project, delete_project, create_task, assign_task, update_task_status, complete_task, delete_task, list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress, add_member, update_member_availability)
      project_data: object containing relevant fields for the command
    Examples:
      {"command": "create_project", "project_data": {"name": "\u7f51\u7ad9\u91cd\u6784", "description": "\u91cd\u6784\u516c\u53f8\u5b98\u7f51", "owner_id": 6}}
  
  OUTPUT FORMAT:
    Format: json
    Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
    Schema:
      success: boolean
      message: string
      data: object (varies by command)
    Examples:
      {"success": true, "message": "\u9879\u76ee\u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f", "data": {"project_id": 1, "status": "planning"}}
  
  Functional Requirements:
  [FR-001] Create Project: Create a new project with name, description, owner_id. Check owner exists. Default status planning. Record creation time.
  [FR-002] Update Project: Update project info: description, owner_id, status, deadline. Check project exists. Record status changes.
  [FR-003] Delete Project: Delete project and all its tasks. Check project exists.
  [FR-004] Create Task: Create task with project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours. Check project exists and status is planning or active. Default status todo. No assignee.
  [FR-005] Assign Task: Assign task to a member. Check task exists and unassigned or reassignable. Check member exists and available. Optionally check skills. Update assignee_id and member status to busy.
  [FR-006] Update Task Status: Update task status. Check task exists. Status flow: todo->in_progress->review->done. Blocked can be set anytime. Record actual_hours when done.
  [FR-007] Complete Task: Complete a task from review to done. Record actual_hours. Release member (set available).
  [FR-008] Delete Task: Delete a task. Check task exists. If assigned, release member.
  [FR-009] List Project Tasks: List tasks for a project with optional filters: status, priority, assignee. Show task details and progress.
  [FR-010] Get Member Tasks: Get all tasks for a member: current and historical. Calculate total hours.
  [FR-011] Get Project Progress: Get project progress: completion rate, task counts by status, estimated vs actual hours, estimated completion time.
  [FR-012] Add Member: Add a new team member. Check member_id uniqueness.
  [FR-013] Update Member Availability: Update member availability. Can only set to offline if no active tasks.

Parent Constraints:
  - {'constraint_id': 'TC-STORAGE', 'description': 'Storage: memory - Three independent in-memory data sources: members, projects, tasks.'}
  - {'constraint_id': 'TC-CONCURRENCY', 'description': 'Concurrency: single-user, auth_required: False'}
  - {'constraint_id': 'TC-UI', 'description': 'UI: api'}
  - {'constraint_id': 'TC-LANGUAGE', 'description': 'Language: Python'}

Parent Traces to: FR-001, FR-002, FR-003, FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, FR-008, FR-009, FR-010, FR-011, FR-012, FR-013

Parent Inputs:
  - input: Any - System input
Parent Outputs:
  - output: Any - System output

Data Sources (Available Data Stores):
  - members (memory, read_write)
    Type: list
    Description: Stores team member information.
  - projects (memory, read_write)
    Type: list
    Description: Stores project information.
  - tasks (memory, read_write)
    Type: list
    Description: Stores task records.

Global Variables:
  - read_write on members: Stores team member information.
  - read_write on projects: Stores project information.
  - read_write on tasks: Stores task records.

============================================================
CHILDREN - INTERFACES AND DATA OPERATIONS
============================================================

DECOMPOSITION RATIONALE (how children collaborate):
The parent Project_prd receives a JSON input with command and project_data. First, ParseInput extracts command and project_data. Then RouteCommand dispatches to the appropriate handler based on the command. There are 8 handlers: HandleCreateProject, HandleUpdateProject, HandleDeleteProject, HandleCreateTask, HandleAssignTask, HandleUpdateTaskStatus, HandleCompleteTask, HandleDeleteTask. Each handler performs its specific operation using the data sources. The remaining commands (list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress, add_member, update_member_availability) are not included due to the limit of 10 children; they would be additional handlers. The decomposition covers all functional requirements except FR-009 to FR-013, which would require additional children. The dataflow is linear: ParseInput -> RouteCommand -> handler -> result back to RouteCommand -> parent output. Each handler's inputs come from project_data, and outputs are returned to RouteCommand.

Child Functions (interfaces only, not implemented):

  [ParseInput]
    Purpose: Parse the input JSON to extract command and project_data.
    Behavior: Parses the input JSON string into a dictionary, then extracts the 'command' and 'project_data' fields. Returns them as separate outputs.
    Signature: def ParseInput(input: Any) -> Tuple[str, dict]
    Preconditions: ['Input is valid JSON']
    Postconditions: ['command and project_data are extracted']
    Acceptance: Parses valid JSON and returns command and project_data

  [RouteCommand]
    Purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler function.
    Behavior: Uses a dictionary mapping command strings to handler functions. Calls the appropriate handler with project_data and returns its result. If command is unknown, returns error.
    Signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, project_data: dict) -> dict
    Preconditions: ['command is one of the valid commands']
    Postconditions: ['Result is produced by the appropriate handler']
    Acceptance: Routes known commands to correct handler
    Acceptance: Returns error for unknown command

  [HandleCreateProject]
    Purpose: Handle create_project command: create a new project.
    Behavior: Checks if owner exists via members.exists. If not, return error. Otherwise, create project with fields: name, description, owner_id, status='planning', created_at=current time. Return success with project_id and status.
    Signature: def HandleCreateProject(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - members: read (Check owner existence)
      - projects: write (Create new project)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains name, description, owner_id']
    Postconditions: ['Project is created if owner exists']
    Acceptance: Create project with valid owner returns success
    Acceptance: Create project with invalid owner returns failure
    Traces: FR-001

  [HandleUpdateProject]
    Purpose: Handle update_project command: update project info.
    Behavior: Check project exists via projects.exists. If not, return error. Update allowed fields: description, owner_id, status, deadline. If status changes, record change (e.g., in a log or update timestamp). Return success with updated project.
    Signature: def HandleUpdateProject(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - projects: read_write (Check existence and update project)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains project_id']
    Postconditions: ['Project is updated if exists']
    Acceptance: Update existing project returns success
    Acceptance: Update non-existing project returns failure
    Traces: FR-002

  [HandleDeleteProject]
    Purpose: Handle delete_project command: delete project and all its tasks.
    Behavior: Check project exists. If not, return error. List all tasks for the project and delete each. Then delete the project. Return success.
    Signature: def HandleDeleteProject(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - projects: read_write (Check existence and delete project)
      - tasks: read_write (List and delete tasks)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains project_id']
    Postconditions: ['Project and its tasks are deleted if project exists']
    Acceptance: Delete existing project and its tasks returns success
    Acceptance: Delete non-existing project returns failure
    Traces: FR-003

  [HandleCreateTask]
    Purpose: Handle create_task command: create a new task.
    Behavior: Check project exists via projects.get. If not, return error. Check project status is 'planning' or 'active'. If not, return error. Create task with fields: project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours, status='todo', assignee_id=None, created_at=current time. Return success with task details.
    Signature: def HandleCreateTask(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - projects: read (Check project existence and status)
      - tasks: write (Create new task)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours']
    Postconditions: ['Task is created if project exists and status is planning or active']
    Acceptance: Create task in active project returns success
    Acceptance: Create task in non-existing project returns failure
    Traces: FR-004

  [HandleAssignTask]
    Purpose: Handle assign_task command: assign task to a member.
    Behavior: Check task exists via tasks.get. If not, return error. Check task assignee_id is None or reassignable (e.g., status not done). Check member exists via members.get. If not, return error. Check member availability (status should be 'available'). If not, return error. Optionally check skills (if required). Update task assignee_id to member_id. Update member status to 'busy'. Return success.
    Signature: def HandleAssignTask(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - tasks: read_write (Check task and update assignee)
      - members: read_write (Check member and update status)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains task_id, member_id']
    Postconditions: ['Task is assigned and member status is busy if conditions met']
    Acceptance: Assign task to available member returns success
    Acceptance: Assign task to busy member returns failure
    Traces: FR-005

  [HandleUpdateTaskStatus]
    Purpose: Handle update_task_status command: update task status.
    Behavior: Check task exists. If not, return error. Validate status transition: allowed transitions: todo->in_progress, in_progress->review, review->done. Blocked can be set anytime. If new status is 'done', require actual_hours and record it. Update task status and optionally actual_hours. Return success.
    Signature: def HandleUpdateTaskStatus(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - tasks: read_write (Check task and update status)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains task_id, status']
    Postconditions: ['Task status is updated if valid']
    Acceptance: Update task status from todo to in_progress succeeds
    Acceptance: Invalid transition returns failure
    Traces: FR-006

  [HandleCompleteTask]
    Purpose: Handle complete_task command: complete a task from review to done.
    Behavior: Check task exists and status is 'review'. If not, return error. Update task status to 'done', set actual_hours. If task has assignee_id, get member and set status to 'available'. Return success.
    Signature: def HandleCompleteTask(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - tasks: read_write (Check and update task)
      - members: read_write (Release member if assigned)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains task_id, actual_hours']
    Postconditions: ['Task is completed and member is released if assigned']
    Acceptance: Complete task from review sets status done and releases member
    Traces: FR-007

  [HandleDeleteTask]
    Purpose: Handle delete_task command: delete a task.
    Behavior: Check task exists. If not, return error. If task has assignee_id, get member and set status to 'available'. Delete the task. Return success.
    Signature: def HandleDeleteTask(project_data: dict) -> dict
    Data Operations:
      - tasks: read_write (Check and delete task)
      - members: read_write (Release member if assigned)
    Preconditions: ['project_data contains task_id']
    Postconditions: ['Task is deleted and member released if assigned']
    Acceptance: Delete task releases member if assigned
    Traces: FR-008

Global Variables:
  - read_write on members: Stores team member information.
  - read_write on projects: Stores project information.
  - read_write on tasks: Stores task records.

============================================================
DATA SCHEMA REFERENCE (use these EXACT field names when accessing dict keys)
============================================================
  members dict fields:
    - member_id: integer
    - name: string
    - role: string
    - skills: list of strings
    - availability: string
    (invariant) member_id is unique
    (invariant) availability is one of available, busy, offline
  projects dict fields:
    - project_id: integer
    - name: string
    - description: string
    - owner_id: integer
    - status: string
    - created_at: datetime
    - deadline: datetime
    (invariant) project_id is unique
    (invariant) status is one of planning, active, completed, cancelled
  tasks dict fields:
    - task_id: integer
    - project_id: integer
    - title: string
    - description: string
    - assignee_id: integer
    - status: string
    - priority: string
    - estimated_hours: number
    - actual_hours: number
    - created_at: datetime
    (invariant) task_id is unique
    (invariant) status is one of todo, in_progress, review, done, blocked
    (invariant) priority is one of low, medium, high, critical

>>> When accessing a dict field, ALWAYS use the exact field name from the schema above. <<<
>>> If a schema is listed under 'orders dict fields', then 'order' should use those fields. <<<


============================================================
INTERFACE ENFORCEMENT - LOCKED SIGNATURE
============================================================
Your exact function signature is LOCKED and MUST be:
  def Project_prd(input: Any) -> Any:
Do NOT change parameter names, types, or return type.
Adding, removing, or renaming parameters will cause verification failure.

============================================================
IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS
============================================================
1. Generate ONLY a function definition, no classes
2. Call the child functions with correct arguments
3. Handle the return values from child functions
4. Return a result that matches the parent outputs
